subtext: Because we had to do it

questions.text: Which botanical family contains true yams?
What family does the sweet potato belong to?
What is the scientific name for the most commonly cultivated true yam species?
Which is a common visual characteristic of many true yams?

answers.B: Smooth, red skin with orange flesh

questions.text: What is the fundamental botanical difference between true yams and sweet potatoes?

answers.A: True yams are roots, sweet potatoes are stems
answers.B: True yams form tubers (modified stems), sweet potatoes form storage roots
answers.C: True yams grow underground, sweet potatoes grow on vines above ground
answers.D: True yams are annuals, sweet potatoes are perennials

questions.text: What is the primary reason for confusion between sweet potatoes and yams in North America?

answers.B: The term "yam" was historically used for marketing orange-fleshed sweet potatoes
answers.C: They can cross-pollinate to create hybrids
answers.D: They were classified as the same species until 1985

questions.text: Which characteristic best differentiates the internal texture of a true yam from a sweet potato?

answers.D: Yams are typically drier and starchier

questions.text: What nutritional difference is typically found between true yams and sweet potatoes?

answers.A: Sweet potatoes are higher in beta-carotene
answers.D: Yams are a significant source of vitamin C

questions.text: Which feature identifies a plant as a true yam (genus Dioscorea)?

answers.A: Belongs to genus Dioscorea and forms tubers

questions.text: How do true yams differ from sweet potatoes in their growth cycle?

answers.A: Yams mature in 30 days, sweet potatoes take 120 days
answers.B: Yams typically require a longer growing season than sweet potatoes
answers.C: Sweet potatoes can only be planted in spring, yams in fall
answers.D: Yams produce fruit above ground, sweet potatoes do not

questions.text: Which statement is correct regarding the origin of true yams versus sweet potatoes?

answers.C: Sweet potatoes originated in Africa, yams in South America
answers.D: True yams originated primarily in Africa and Asia, sweet potatoes in the Americas

questions.text: Which yam species is the most widely cultivated in West Africa?
Which yam species is known as "cush-cush" in parts of the Caribbean?
Which yam species is native to Asia and known for its large size and purple flesh varieties?
What yam variety is known as "air potato" or "aerial yam" due to its distinctive growth habit?
Which yam species is known as the "bitter yam" and requires special processing to remove toxins?
What variety of yam is commonly called "Chinese yam" or "cinnamon vine"?
Which of the following yam species is native to the Amazon region?
What is the common name for Dioscorea cayenensis?
Why is Dioscorea alata commonly called "winged yam"?

answers.A: Because of its wing-like ridges on the stems
answers.B: Because the tuber has wing-like projections
answers.D: Because the flowers have wing-like petals

questions.text: What physical characteristic is most commonly used to identify different yam cultivars?

answers.B: Tuber shape, size, flesh color, and skin characteristics

questions.text: Which describes the flesh of purple yam (ube, Dioscorea alata)?
What is a distinguishing characteristic of the Yellow Guinea yam (Dioscorea cayenensis)?

answers.D: Grows only on trees, never underground

questions.text: Which yam cultivar is known for its exceptionally long tubers that can grow to over 1 meter in length?
What unique characteristic identifies the Trifoliate yam (Dioscorea dumetorum)?

answers.C: Blue flowers that bloom at night

questions.text: Which yam cultivar is identified by its small, clustered tubers rather than a single large tuber?
What characteristic of yam cultivars often correlates with cooking time?
Which term describes yam cultivars that produce aerial tubers in leaf axils?
What feature distinguishes the Eboe yam from other White Guinea yam cultivars?

answers.D: Ability to grow in salt water

questions.text: Which characteristic helps identify the Japanese mountain yam (Dioscorea japonica)?
What defines a yam variety as "heirloom"?

answers.A: It must be officially registered with a government agency
answers.B: It must be less than 10 years old
answers.C: It has been grown for multiple generations and passed down through families
answers.D: It must have a specific flavor profile determined by a tasting panel

questions.text: Which specialty yam variety is prized in Japanese cuisine for its slimy texture when grated?
What is "ube" in Filipino cuisine?

answers.B: A purple-fleshed yam used in desserts

questions.text: Which heirloom yam variety is traditionally cultivated in the Pacific Islands and has significant cultural importance?
What characteristic makes the Cush-Cush yam (Dioscorea trifida) a specialty variety?

answers.A: It grows only at elevations above 5,000 feet
answers.B: It can be harvested after only 30 days
answers.C: Its fine texture and exceptional flavor

questions.text: Which yam species requires virus-free planting material due to its susceptibility to yam mosaic virus?
What distinguishes "old-fashioned" yam varieties from modern commercial cultivars?

answers.B: Traditional varieties often have more diversity in flavor and texture
answers.D: Old varieties cannot be stored for more than one week

questions.text: Which specialty yam variety contains naturally occurring anthocyanins that give it a distinctive color?
How are yams primarily classified botanically?

answers.A: By genus and species in the family Dioscoreaceae

questions.text: What is the basis for the traditional West African classification system for yams?

answers.C: Characteristics such as maturity period, tuber shape, and culinary properties

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a common basis for farmers' classification of yam varieties?
What modern approach has been used to verify traditional yam classification systems?
Which organization has established international standards for yam germplasm classification?
How are cultivated yam varieties typically classified in agricultural systems?

answers.C: By a combination of species, agronomic traits, and local names

questions.text: What classification challenge is common with traditional yam landraces?

answers.B: Similar varieties may have different local names in different regions
answers.C: They cannot be grown in experimental stations
answers.D: They are always immune to all diseases

questions.text: Which classification approach combines traditional farmer knowledge with scientific analysis?
What system is used to maintain records of yam genetic resources in germplasm banks?

answers.A: Accession numbers linked to passport data

questions.text: What soil type is most suitable for yam cultivation?

answers.D: Alkaline soil with high salt content

questions.text: What is the optimal soil pH range for yam cultivation?
What traditional soil preparation technique is specifically used for yam cultivation in West Africa?
How deep should soil be tilled for optimal yam production?
Which of the following is NOT recommended in soil preparation for yams?

answers.D: Breaking up clods and removing stones

questions.text: What role do mounds play in traditional yam cultivation?

answers.A: They provide good drainage and loose soil for tuber expansion

questions.text: What is the optimal soil organic matter content for yam production?
Which practice helps maintain soil fertility in yam production systems?
What traditional soil conservation technique is often paired with yam cultivation in tropical regions?
What should be avoided when selecting a site for yam cultivation?
What is the primary planting material used for yam propagation?
What is the recommended size for yam setts (pieces) used as planting material?
What treatment is commonly applied to yam setts before planting to prevent rot and disease?

answers.C: Exposure to direct sunlight for 48 hours

questions.text: What is "milking" in yam cultivation?

answers.C: Harvesting tubers while leaving the root system intact for a second harvest
answers.D: The first stage of yam processing

questions.text: What is the optimal planting depth for yam setts?
Which innovative technique is used to produce clean planting material for yams?

answers.B: Using only wild yams as planting material

questions.text: What is the recommended spacing between yam mounds or ridges in traditional cultivation?
What planting system is used to maximize yam yield in small spaces?

answers.C: Stake or trellis systems for vine support

questions.text: Which part of the yam tuber is preferred for use as planting material?

answers.D: Only the skin, removing all flesh

questions.text: What characteristic of yam setts indicates they are suitable for planting?

answers.D: Presence of viable buds or sprouts

questions.text: What is the purpose of pre-sprouting yam setts before field planting?

answers.A: To identify viable planting material and ensure uniform emergence
answers.B: To reduce the size of the harvested tubers

questions.text: What is the typical length of the growth cycle for most cultivated yam species?
What is the first visible stage of yam development after planting setts?
During what stage of growth do yams begin significant tuber enlargement?

answers.B: After vine establishment, typically 2-3 months after planting
answers.D: During the last week before harvest

questions.text: Which factors play a role in triggering tuber initiation in yams?

answers.D: Multiple factors including photoperiod, temperature, and vine maturity

questions.text: What happens to yam vines toward the end of the growth cycle?

answers.D: They detach and form new plants

questions.text: What is the relationship between vine growth and tuber development in yams?

answers.A: Healthy vine growth is necessary before significant tuber bulking occurs
answers.B: Vines and tubers grow at exactly the same rate
answers.C: Vines grow only after tubers are fully formed
answers.D: Vine growth and tuber growth are completely independent

questions.text: What growth characteristic of yam vines requires management in cultivation?

answers.B: Their climbing nature and need for support
answers.D: Their inability to grow more than 30 cm tall

questions.text: Which statement best describes yam root system development?

answers.A: Yams develop only a single tap root
answers.B: Yams have no roots, only tubers
answers.C: Yam roots grow only from the tuber surface
answers.D: Yams develop adventitious roots from the planted sett and from nodes on new stems

questions.text: What physiological process marks the transition from vegetative growth to storage organ development in yams?

answers.A: Photoassimilate partitioning shift from vines to tubers
answers.D: Color change in the leaves from green to red

questions.text: Which nematode species is most commonly associated with dry rot disease of yam tubers?
What is the primary symptom of anthracnose disease in yams?
Which virus is considered the most damaging to yam crops?
What is the most effective cultural practice to manage yam beetles?
Which field pest can cause significant losses to yam tubers by boring into them underground?
What is the appropriate management approach for yam nematode prevention?

answers.C: Using clean planting material and crop rotation

questions.text: Which fungal pathogen causes storage rot (soft rot) in yam tubers?
Which integrated pest management technique is used for controlling yam diseases?

answers.A: Calendar-based weekly pesticide application regardless of pest presence
answers.B: Removing all wildlife from the area
answers.C: Planting in exactly the same location each year
answers.D: Using resistant varieties combined with cultural controls

questions.text: What fungi causes tuber rot during yam storage?
Which of the following is NOT an effective traditional practice for managing yam pests?

answers.D: Using wood ash on planting material

questions.text: What is the most environmentally sustainable approach to yam disease management?

answers.A: Integrated disease management using resistant varieties and cultural practices
answers.D: Complete sterilization of soil before planting

questions.text: What is the primary indicator that yams are ready for harvest?

answers.B: Senescence (yellowing and dying back) of the vines
answers.C: Exactly 90 days after planting regardless of variety

questions.text: How long after planting are most varieties of Dioscorea rotundata (white yam) typically harvested?
What traditional technique is used to determine if yams are mature enough for harvest?

answers.A: Carefully exposing the top of the tuber to check its size
answers.C: Counting the exact number of leaves

questions.text: What time of year are yams typically harvested in tropical West Africa?

answers.D: Throughout the year regardless of season

questions.text: What happens to yam tuber quality if harvesting is significantly delayed after maturity?

answers.B: They may become fibrous or begin to rot
answers.D: They develop an ability to photosynthesize

questions.text: Which factor most significantly affects the timing of yam harvests in traditional farming systems?
What harvest timing consideration is specific to water yams (Dioscorea alata)?

answers.A: They can have a longer growing season than white yams
answers.B: They must be harvested before sunrise
answers.C: They must be harvested during rainfall
answers.D: They can only be harvested during full moons

questions.text: Which visual indicator in the field helps farmers determine yam harvest timing?

answers.A: Appearance of flowers on nearby trees
answers.C: Senescence of the vines and drying of leaves
answers.D: Change in soil color from brown to red

questions.text: What harvest timing practice is used in the "milking" system of yam cultivation?

answers.A: Harvesting only during morning hours when dew is present
answers.B: Early partial harvest followed by a final harvest at full maturity
answers.C: Harvesting only female plants first, followed by male plants

questions.text: What traditional tool is commonly used for harvesting yams in smallholder farming systems?
What technique helps minimize damage to yam tubers during harvesting?

answers.A: Carefully removing soil from around the tuber before lifting
answers.B: Pulling directly on the vines with maximum force
answers.D: Applying heat to the soil surface

questions.text: Why is care taken to avoid cuts and bruises when harvesting yams?

answers.B: To prevent the release of toxic gases
answers.D: To prevent infection and reduce storage losses

questions.text: What characteristic of certain yam species makes them particularly challenging to harvest?

answers.A: Their tendency to explode upon contact with air
answers.B: Their depth and potential length (some growing more than 1 meter deep)
answers.C: Their ability to move through the soil

questions.text: Which approach is recommended when harvesting yams in heavy clay soils?

answers.B: Harvesting only during the hottest part of the day
answers.C: Loosening soil carefully to avoid breaking the tubers
answers.D: Using explosives to break up the soil

questions.text: What should be done with the yam vines during harvest?

answers.A: Removed and used for mulch or compost
answers.C: Left attached to the tubers during storage

questions.text: How does mechanized yam harvesting differ from traditional methods?

answers.B: It requires planting yams on trees
answers.C: It can only be done during winter months
answers.D: It uses adapted diggers that lift and expose tubers for collection

questions.text: What post-harvest field management practice is recommended in yam cultivation systems?

answers.A: Immediate replanting of yams in the same mounds
answers.B: Planting a rotation crop or cover crop
answers.C: Leaving the field completely bare for at least one year
answers.D: Flooding the field for six months

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of curing yams after harvest?

answers.A: To heal wounds and form a protective corky layer on the skin

questions.text: What are the optimal conditions for curing freshly harvested yams?
How long does the typical yam curing process take?
Which physiological process occurs during proper yam curing?
What traditional method is used for curing yams in West Africa?

answers.A: Leaving tubers in piles or beds covered with yam vines
answers.C: Exposing to direct sunlight for 72 hours

questions.text: What should be avoided during the yam curing process?
How does proper curing affect yam storage life?

answers.A: It reduces storage life but improves flavor
answers.B: It increases storage life by reducing water loss and decay
answers.C: It has no effect on storage but improves color
answers.D: It reduces nutritional content while improving texture

questions.text: What physical change indicates that yams have been properly cured?

answers.D: Formation of a corky layer over cuts and wounds

questions.text: Which approach to curing is used in commercial yam production systems?
What is the relationship between curing temperature and humidity?

answers.A: They must be inversely related (high temperature, low humidity)
answers.C: Both should be relatively high during curing
answers.D: Temperature should be below freezing with 100% humidity

questions.text: Which process actively continues in yam tubers during the curing period?
What is the traditional yam storage structure used in West Africa?

answers.C: Yam barn with thatched roof and open sides

questions.text: What is the optimal temperature range for long-term yam storage?
Why should yams NOT be stored with fruits like apples and bananas?

answers.D: Ethylene produced by fruits accelerates yam sprouting

questions.text: What is the typical maximum storage duration for properly cured and stored yams?
Which of the following conditions should be avoided in yam storage areas?

answers.C: High humidity combined with poor ventilation

questions.text: What storage technique is used to prevent rodent damage to stored yams?

answers.A: Hanging individual tubers or using raised platforms with rat guards

questions.text: What causes the green discoloration that sometimes develops in stored yams?

answers.D: Exposure to light and development of chlorophyll

questions.text: Which physiological process continues during yam storage and affects storage duration?
Which method is used for commercial long-term storage of seed yams?
What key factor affects the storage life of different yam species?

answers.A: Dormancy period characteristic to each species

questions.text: What is the most important design feature of a yam storage facility?

answers.B: Good ventilation and protection from rain

questions.text: Which traditional yam storage method is used in parts of Southeast Asia?

answers.D: Storage in underground pits lined with rice straw

questions.text: What feature helps regulate temperature in traditional yam barns?

answers.A: Thatched roof and raised, slatted shelves

questions.text: How are yams typically arranged in traditional storage structures?

answers.C: Tied or placed on shelves in single layers

questions.text: What adaptations are made to yam storage facilities in areas with termite problems?

answers.B: Treatment of wooden structures or use of termite-resistant materials
answers.C: Keeping lights on 24 hours per day

questions.text: What consideration is important when designing a commercial yam storage facility?

answers.C: Must be within sight of a body of water
answers.D: Must accommodate inspection, sorting, and rotation of stock

questions.text: What traditional practice improves pest management in yam storage structures?

answers.A: Using smoke or plant materials with pest-repellent properties
answers.C: Painting geometric patterns on the exterior

questions.text: Which feature is recommended for modern yam storage facilities in humid tropical regions?
How does yam arrangement affect storage success in traditional yam barns?

answers.A: Yams must be arranged alphabetically by variety name
answers.B: Yams should be arranged to allow air circulation between tubers
answers.C: Yams must be stacked at least 12 layers high
answers.D: Yams should be arranged by color from light to dark

questions.text: What is the primary macronutrient in yams?
What is the approximate protein content of yams per 100g of edible portion?
Which vitamin is present in significant amounts in most yam varieties?
What mineral is notably present in yams, contributing to their nutritional value?
How does the caloric content of yams compare to that of white potatoes of equal weight?

answers.A: Yams have approximately twice the calories
answers.B: Yams have about half the calories
answers.C: Yams have exactly the same caloric content
answers.D: Yams have slightly more calories on average

questions.text: Which nutritional component gives purple yam (ube) its distinctive color?
What is the typical moisture content of fresh yams?
Which statement best describes the fat content of yams?

answers.C: Very low in all types of fat

questions.text: What type of carbohydrate primarily comprises the starch in yams?
Which nutrient in yams contributes to their role in digestive health?
What property of yams has made them traditionally valuable for women's health in some cultures?

answers.C: Content of diosgenin, a compound similar to female hormones

questions.text: Which health benefit is associated with the high potassium content in yams?
What digestive health benefit is provided by yams?

answers.B: They provide dietary fiber for digestive regularity
answers.D: They directly kill harmful intestinal parasites

questions.text: Which antioxidant compounds found in purple yams may contribute to their health benefits?
What makes yams a good food choice for blood sugar management?

answers.A: They have a lower glycemic index than many other starchy foods
answers.D: They eliminate the need for insulin in diabetics

questions.text: What nutrient in yams supports immune system function?
Which health benefit is linked to the manganese content of yams?

answers.B: Support for bone health and metabolism

questions.text: What nutritional advantage do yams offer for people seeking weight management?

answers.D: They provide nutrient density with relatively low caloric content

questions.text: Which traditional use of wild yam should NOT be considered scientifically validated?

answers.A: As a complete substitute for hormone replacement therapy
answers.B: As a source of complex carbohydrates
answers.C: As a dietary source of vitamins
answers.D: As a food that can be included in a balanced diet

questions.text: Which bioactive compounds found in certain yam species have been studied for their anti-inflammatory properties?
What characteristic of yam starch has made it beneficial for certain digestive conditions?

answers.A: It has a sour flavor that stimulates digestion
answers.B: It can be easily digested by people with certain digestive sensitivities
answers.D: It eliminates the need for chewing

questions.text: Which dietary pattern traditionally incorporates yams as a staple food?
How should bitter varieties of yam be prepared to make them safe for consumption?

answers.A: They should never be consumed under any circumstances
answers.B: They should be eaten raw to maximize nutritional content
answers.C: They require special processing to remove toxic compounds
answers.D: They should be consumed only during winter months

questions.text: What consideration is important when incorporating yams into a low-sodium diet?

answers.A: Yams are naturally low in sodium and suitable for low-sodium diets
answers.B: Yams must be soaked in salt water before consumption
answers.D: Yams can only be consumed with high-sodium seasonings

questions.text: How do yams fit into a gluten-free diet?

answers.A: They contain minimal gluten that must be removed by special processing
answers.B: They are naturally gluten-free and safe for people with celiac disease
answers.C: They contain gluten only in the skin

questions.text: What dietary consideration is important when preparing yams with their skins?

answers.A: The skins are indigestible and must be removed
answers.B: The skins contain toxic levels of cyanide
answers.C: The skins must be consumed within 2 hours of peeling
answers.D: The skins should be thoroughly cleaned to remove soil contaminants

questions.text: How do yams fit into a vegetarian or vegan diet?

answers.A: They are acceptable only for vegetarians but not vegans
answers.B: They require animal products for proper preparation
answers.C: They are plant-based and suitable for both vegetarian and vegan diets
answers.D: They are classified as animal products due to their protein content

questions.text: What consideration is important when incorporating yams into a diabetic meal plan?

answers.A: Portion control and accounting for carbohydrate content
answers.B: Complete avoidance due to high sugar content
answers.D: Yams must be consumed raw to be suitable for diabetics

questions.text: How can yams be incorporated into a weight management diet?

answers.A: They should be avoided completely due to high calorie content
answers.B: They can be included in moderate portions as a nutritious complex carbohydrate
answers.C: They should be consumed only in liquid form
answers.D: They must be eaten before 10:00 AM to avoid weight gain

questions.text: What is the first step in preparing most yam dishes?
Why should gloves be worn when peeling some varieties of yams?

answers.A: They may cause skin irritation due to calcium oxalate crystals

questions.text: What is the most common basic cooking method for yams worldwide?
What is "fufu" in West African cuisine?

answers.B: A pounded yam paste eaten with stews
answers.C: Yam leaves prepared as a salad

questions.text: How should yams be stored after peeling but before cooking?

answers.A: In warm water at room temperature
answers.B: Exposed to air for at least 12 hours
answers.C: In cool water with lemon juice or vinegar to prevent browning
answers.D: Wrapped in aluminum foil and heated

questions.text: What precaution should be taken when preparing bitter varieties of yams?

answers.A: Extended boiling with multiple water changes
answers.C: Consumption limited to one tablespoon daily
answers.D: Exposure to ultraviolet light for 24 hours

questions.text: Which cooking method best preserves the nutritional content of yams?
What technique is used to make yam "chips" or crisps?

answers.B: Thinly slicing and deep frying or baking

questions.text: What preparation method is used to make "poi" from taro in Pacific Island cuisines?

answers.C: Cooking and pounding into a fermented paste
answers.D: Juicing and boiling down to a syrup

questions.text: What is the Nigerian dish "asaro"?

answers.B: Yam porridge cooked with palm oil and peppers

questions.text: What starch is traditionally used to make Jamaican bammy?
Which Caribbean dish combines yams with other starchy vegetables and meat in a boiled preparation?
What is the Japanese dish "tororo"?

answers.C: Grated mountain yam with a slimy texture

questions.text: How is Korean "ma" commonly consumed?

answers.A: Blended into a drink or served cooked/steamed
answers.D: Freezing and eating like ice cream

questions.text: What is "mpotompoto" in Ghanaian cuisine?
Which Filipino dessert prominently features purple yam?
What is the main ingredient in the Chinese dessert "taro cake" (wu tao gou)?

answers.C: Taro root (closely related to but distinct from true yams)

questions.text: What preparation technique is used for the Indian dish "senai kilangu varuval"?
Which technique is essential in preparing the Vietnamese dish "canh khoai mỡ"?

answers.D: Simmering in a soup with pork

questions.text: Which West African dish features boiled yam pounded into a smooth, stretchy fufu and served with soup?
What characteristic of purple yam (ube) makes it popular for desserts?
Which Filipino dessert combines purple yam with coconut milk in a sweet pudding?
How are yams typically prepared for use in sweet pies?
Which purple tuber commonly used in Okinawan desserts is actually a sweet potato, not a true yam?
Which Japanese confection traditionally uses grated yam mixed with rice flour?
How is yam incorporated into ice cream production?

answers.A: Cooked, puréed, and used as flavoring base
answers.C: Used only as a natural coloring that adds no flavor
answers.D: Fermented first, then added as chunks

questions.text: What preparation technique is used to create candied yams?

answers.D: Baking with sugar, butter, and spices

questions.text: Which sweet yam preparation is traditional in Thailand?

answers.B: Boiled in syrup with coconut milk
answers.D: Raw yam with honey dipping sauce

questions.text: What makes certain yam varieties better suited for dessert applications?

answers.C: Natural sweetness and appropriate starch structure

questions.text: How are yams incorporated into baked goods like cakes and brownies?

answers.A: Cooked, puréed, and added to batter
answers.D: Used only as decoration on top

questions.text: What is the traditional West African preparation "ikokore"?

answers.B: Grated water yam formed into balls and cooked in soup

questions.text: Which cooking method is commonly used for yam chips or fries?
How are yams typically incorporated into stews?

answers.A: Cut into chunks and added to cook with the stew
answers.B: Puréed and used as thickener only
answers.C: Added only as garnish at the end
answers.D: Used only for flavoring the cooking liquid then removed

questions.text: What preparation technique is used for the Nigerian dish "ojojo"?

answers.A: Boiling whole yams with the skin
answers.B: Roasting yam pieces over open flame

questions.text: Which savory yam dish features yam cooked in coconut milk?
What is the role of yam in the Ghanaian dish "mpotompoto"?

answers.C: Main ingredient in a savory porridge

questions.text: What is the main ingredient in the Brazilian dish "caruru"?
What technique is used to prepare yams for the Caribbean dish "tostones de ñame"?

answers.D: Slicing, frying, smashing, and frying again

questions.text: What is the approximate boiling time for 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes of yam?
What technique helps prevent oxidation (browning) of cut yams?

answers.D: Immersion in water with lemon juice or vinegar

questions.text: Which cooking technique is used to create the crispy exterior in fried yam dishes?

answers.A: Patting dry before frying and using proper oil temperature

questions.text: What is the traditional pounding technique used to create "fufu" from boiled yams?

answers.A: Using a food processor with ice
answers.B: Using a rolling pin on a flat surface
answers.C: Using a wooden mortar and pestle

questions.text: What cooking technique best preserves the shape of yam pieces in soups and stews?

answers.B: Adding later in the cooking process

questions.text: What technique is used to achieve the gluey, stretchy texture desired in some Asian yam dishes?
What technique is used to reduce the sliminess of certain yam varieties?

answers.A: Soaking in salt water before cooking

questions.text: Which cooking method best develops the natural sweetness of yams?
What technique is used in creating twice-cooked yam dishes?

answers.B: Cooking once, cooling, then cooking again with different method
answers.C: Cooking once in water, once in milk
answers.D: Cooking at sunrise and again at sunset on the same day

questions.text: Which country is the world's largest producer of yams?
Approximately what percentage of global yam production comes from West Africa?
What factor most significantly limits international trade in fresh yams?

answers.D: Short shelf life and storage challenges

questions.text: Which value-added yam product has seen the greatest growth in international trade?

answers.B: Yam flour and processed yam products

questions.text: What organization collects and publishes global yam production statistics?

answers.C: Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations

questions.text: What economic trend has affected yam production in traditional growing regions?

answers.A: Competition from other starchy staples like cassava and rice
answers.B: Increasing demand due to proven cancer-curing properties
answers.D: Complete collapse of all commercial production

questions.text: Which market segment has shown increased demand for specialty yam varieties?

answers.D: Health food and ethnic cuisine markets

questions.text: What factor most significantly affects year-to-year market prices for yams?

answers.B: Weather conditions and resulting harvest yields

questions.text: What percentage of harvested yams is estimated to be lost to spoilage in developing countries?
What economic role do yams play in traditional West African societies?

answers.A: Staple food crop and sometimes a form of currency or wealth indicator
answers.B: Purely decorative with no practical use

questions.text: Why are yams particularly important for food security in some regions?

answers.A: They grow well underwater during floods
answers.B: They can be harvested daily throughout the year
answers.D: They can be stored for several months without refrigeration

questions.text: What technique do subsistence farmers use to maintain yam planting material from year to year?

questions.text: How do yams contribute to household food security in traditional farming systems?

questions.text: What social role do yam festivals play in traditional yam-growing communities?

questions.text: What constraint most limits yam production expansion by subsistence farmers?

answers.A: Many cultivated varieties rarely flower or produce viable seeds

questions.text: What historical role did yams play in traditional West African societies?
